sutta: now the house father anata pindika came to visit the exalted 1 as he said at 1 side the exalted 1 said this to him house father there are these 4 conditions desirable which are desirable dear delightful heart to win in the world what for the wish o may wealth by law lawful means come to me this is the first condition wealth being gotten by lawful means may good reputation attend me along with my kinsmen and teachers this is the second condition wealth being gotten by lawful means and good reputation being gotten by me along with my kinsmen and teachers may i live long and reach a great age this is the third condition then wealth being gotten and good reputation and long life when body breaks up and long life when body breaks up on the other side of death may i attain the happy born the heaven world this is the fourth condition this these house father are the 4 conditions which is desirable dear delightful heart to win in the world i'll just stop here for a while these are the 4 things that lay people normally like they wish for the first 1 that you may get a lot of wealth by lawful names not by lawful means because that will attain a lot of that will entail a lot of problems later but to get a lot of wealth by lawful means the second 1 may you wish that you would have a good reputation the third 1 may you live long the fourth 1 may you have a good rebirth these are the 4 things that most people normally like and then the buddha said now house father to the winning of these 4 conditions 4 other conditions conduce what for perfection of faith perfection of virtue perfection of generosity and perfection of wisdom and what house father is the perfection of faith herein the aryan disciple is faithful he has faith in the enlightenment of the tata thus he it is exalted 1 ar and so forth a buddha is the exalted 1 this house father is called perfection of faith and what is perfection of virtue hearing the aryan disciple abstains from killing from stealing from committing adultery from a lying from drinking fermented liquor which causes lot this is called perfection of virtue and 1 is perfection of generosity herein the aryan disciple lives at home with heart free from the taint of stinginess he is open-handed pure-handed delighting in self-surrender 1 to ask us favor from who delights in dispensing charitable gifts this house father is called perfection of generosity and what is perfection of wisdom [Music] house father living with his heart overcome by coveting and wrongful desire 1 does what he should not feels to do what he should so doing so failing to do he falls from good report and happiness living with his heart overcome by malice by sloth and topper by restlessness and worry by doubt unwavering he does what he should not fails to do what he should so doing so failing to do he falls from good report and happiness now house father that aryan disciple knowing coveting and wrong desire to be a defilement of the mind cast out the mind's defilement of coveting and wrong desire cast out the mind's defilement of malice sloth and topper restlessness and worry doubt and wavering now house father insofar as the aryan disciple knowing coveting and the rest to be defilements of the mind is 1 who has cast out these defilements this aryan disciple is called of great wisdom of far-spread wisdom of clear range of vision 1 perfect in wisdom these 4 conditions house father conduce to the realization of the before said 4 conditions which are desirable dear delightful and hard to win in the world i just stopped here for a while to recapitulate the 4 factors the buddha said now if we want those earlier things which are desirable there are 4 things that we should cultivate the first 1 is faith to our faith in the buddha the second 1 is to have virtue meaning to keep the precepts the 5 precepts third 1 is to be generous 1 who is open-handed likes to to give the fourth 1 is perfection of wisdom this perfection of wisdom is basically to abandon the 5 hindrances to abandon the 5 hindrances means to get samadhi so you see here is very clear that perfection of wisdom is to develop the mind to have samadhi bhavana so that the 5 hindrances are abandoned uh and the 5 hundreds are abandoned as mentioned elsewhere when we get 1 pointedness of mind when the jana is attained and then that is the condition for wisdom now to continue tao house father that same aryan disciple with the wealth acquired by energetic striving amassed by strength of arm won by sweat lawful and lawful and lawfully gotten is the doer of 4 deeds 1 other 4 hearing house father with the wealth acquired by energetic striving and so forth the aryan disciple makes himself happy and cheerful he is a contriver of perfect happiness and makes his mother and father his children and wife his servants and workmen his friends and comrades cheerful and happy he is a contriver of perfect happiness this house father is the first opportunity seized by him turned to married and fittingly made use of then again house father the iron disciple with the wealth acquired by energetic striving amassed by strength of arm won by sweat lawful and lawfully gotten with that wealth he makes himself secure against all misfortunes whatsoever such as may happen by way of fire water the raja a robber an ill-disposed person or an expectant air he takes steps for his defense he makes himself secure this house father is the second opportunity seized by him turned to merit and fittingly made use of again house father with the wealth acquired by energetic striving lawfully gotten the aryan disciple is a maker of the 5-fold offering namely to relatives to guests to departed ghosts to the rajya and to the devas this is the third opportunity seized by him turned to married and fittingly made use of then again house father with the wealth acquired by energetics driving etc the aaron disciple offers a gift to all such recluses and brahmins as abstained from sloth and negligence who are bent on kindness and forbearance who tame the oneself calm the oneself cool the 1 cell to such he offers a gift which has the highest results a gift heavenly resulting in happiness and leading to heaven this is the fourth opportunity seized by him turned to merit and fittingly made use of so how's father the same aryan disciple with the wealth acquired by energetic striving lawfully gotten etc is a doer of these 4 deeds of merit if the wealth of anyone be spent without these 4 deeds of merit such wealth is called wealth that has failed to cease its opportunity fail to win merit unfittingly made use of but house father if the wealth of anyone be spent on these 4 deeds of merit then it is called wealth that has seized its opportunity turned to merit and is fittingly made use of that as the end of the suta
commentary:
now this is a very interesting suta for lay people especially first the buddha is telling us the 4 the the 4 things which everybody wants then secondly the buddha is saying what are the conditions that you must practice to abst to attain what you want in the world then the fourth the third 1 the third set of things the buddha is is trying to tell us how to use your wealth when you have acquired wealth because all the happiness that we get in the world is actually the use of our past merit you know because of our past merit now we have a good life but if you keep on enjoying life you are using up all your good karma so it's very important to put back into your karma bank all the meritorious deeds if you continue to do meritorious deeds you're storing up the good karma in your karma bank so the buddha is is telling us and the first way to use your wealth in skillful way and so that you can get married out of it now is to make yourself happy that means to enjoy life a bit now and all those around you your mother and father wife and children servants and workmen friends and relatives etc you try to make all those around you including yourself a bit happy second 1 you must guard yourself against misfortunes for example fire water these 2 may be nowadays buying insurance or something and then the raja that means yeah you have to be careful in those days and the raja could seize your property so sometimes you have to be nice to the raja buy him a present now and then and then a robber and you have to take steps now so that your house is not dropped maybe get guards or get dogs or something and even an expectant air somebody wants to uh add to your property and some uh in those days they might kill kill that person and so that he can get the the property then the third 1 is to make the fivefold offering to relatives to guests to the departed departed meaning you you do some good deeds and you transfer merit to them but it is mentioned elsewhere in some other suta that they used to offer things to the department and burn them and then to the raja you have to offer to the raja and to the devas devas like offering flowers incense etc so if you do all these things then you conserve your [Applause] good karma so that you will have this uh enjoy the fruits of your good karma again uh it is mentioned elsewhere there's another suta where the buddha said you want the things desirable in the world they are not good to be god by prayers and vows later when we come to that suta i can explain more so here it's not by prayers and vows but the cultivating 4 things faith virtue generosity and wisdom and then i always remember to use your money in a wise way so that it's not wasted use it naturally you get the benefit of it in by way of merit et cetera
